member.office_address: 1714 Longworth House Office Building Washington DC 20515-4100

form_analysis.form_header_text: Rep. Dusty Johnson Fiscal Year 2027 Appropriations Request Form
form_analysis.form_instructions_text: Thank you for your interest in submitting an appropriations request to my congressional office for Fiscal Year (FY) 2027. Below you will find a copy of the submission form for programmatic and language requests for FY2027 that my office will use this year. To properly evaluate a request, the House Appropriations Committee requires certain information: Point of contact information, Applicable Appropriations Subcommittee. For Programmatic requests: Program description, Funding amount requested, FY26 appropriated amount, Funding amount increase/decrease, etc. For Report or Bill Language requests: Language requested, Description/justification, etc. Early submissions are welcome and encouraged. If you have questions, please do not hesitate to contact my office at (202) 225-2801.

sections.description: Information about the organization and person submitting the request.

fields.label: Contact's Mailing Address, City, State, Zip Code
Please tell me how this request will support the constituents of South Dakota.
fields.draft_value: This $9 billion request for the NASA Science Mission Directorate (SMD) sustains over $2.2 million in active local investment. Organizations like South Dakota State University in Brookings and Worthing receive critical grants to advance Moon-to-Mars exploration and applied science, supporting high-tech jobs and the STEM workforce in South Dakota.
fields.rationale: Directly references the local spending total ($2,200,309.11) and identifies South Dakota State University as a key recipient with locations in Brookings and Worthing as found in the NASA spending context.

sections.description: Provide the specific details for your programmatic or language request.

fields.label: Request Type
fields.rationale: The pre-computed reference values identify this as a programmatic request.

fields.label: Previous Programmatic Request�
fields.help_text: Provide details of any previous programmatic request made for this project.
fields.rationale: This field asks for details regarding the submitter's personal history of requests, which is not provided in the context.

fields.label: Description of Program Request
fields.draft_value:
* $9 billion for NASA Science Mission Directorate to restore funding stability after FY26 cuts and ensure mission continuity
* Supports South Dakota State University's $447,547 and $340,992 active grants for Moon-to-Mars exploration and discovery
* Counters China's lunar and planetary expansion; ensures U.S. sets exploration norms through decadal survey priorities
* Sustains SD's high-tech workforce through $2.2M in recent NASA Science obligations; delivers 3:1 ROI for the regional economy
fields.rationale: Requested $9B ask for SMD. Cited specific local spending for SDSU ($780k+ active grants) and the district total ($2.2M). Integrated strategic arguments regarding China competition and economic ROI.

fields.label: Programmatic Request
fields.rationale: This option allows us to advocate for the specific $9 billion topline indicated in the request context.

fields.label: Description of Language Request
fields.rationale: The request type is programmatic according to the context; no language request is required for this funding ask.

fields.label: Exact Language Request
fields.rationale: The request type is programmatic according to the context; no language request is required for this funding ask.

top_grants.description: Execute a balanced science program based on discipline-specific guidance from the national academies of sciences  engineering  and medicine  administration priorities  and direction from congress.   Participate as a key partner and enabler in the agency S exploration initiative  focusing on scientific research of  on  and from the moon  lunar orbit  Mars  and beyond.   Advance discovery in emerging fields by identifying and exploiting cross-disciplinary opportunities between traditional science disciplines   develop a directorate-wide  target-user focused approach to applied programs  including Earth science applications  space weather  planetary defense  and space situational awareness.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Advancing strategic science supporting Moon-to-Mars exploration and defense.
